evals.prompt: Look at `examples/demo-project/red/rose.md`. What project conventions, instructions, or rules should I know before working with this file? For each one you find, state the instruction and which directory/source it comes from.
assertions: Agent mentions the repo-level instruction: 'Keep the resolver deterministic, inspectable, and source-linked'
Agent mentions the project-level instruction: 'Read the project README before editing'
Agent mentions the project-level instruction: 'Prefer small, reversible changes'
Agent mentions the red-level instruction: 'Prefer warm color tones'
Agent mentions the red-level instruction: 'Rose inherits all red conventions'
Agent does NOT mention blue-level instructions (cool tones, violet)

evals.prompt: Look at `examples/demo-project/blue/violet.md`. What project conventions, instructions, or rules should I know before working with this file? For each one, state the instruction and which directory/source it comes from. Are there any other directories nearby with different rules that do not apply here?
assertions: Agent mentions the repo-level instruction: 'Keep the resolver deterministic, inspectable, and source-linked'
Agent mentions the project-level instruction: 'Read the project README before editing'
Agent mentions the blue-level instruction: 'Prefer cool color tones'
Agent mentions the blue-level instruction: 'Violet inherits all blue conventions'
Agent does NOT mention red-level instructions (warm tones, rose)

evals.prompt: I'm about to edit `deno.json` at the root of the zocomputer-agents.md repo. What should I know about this repository's conventions before I start? List each applicable instruction and which source it comes from.
assertions: Agent mentions at least one of the repo-level instructions (design proposal, deterministic resolver, derived context)
Agent does NOT mention project-level instructions (read README, reversible changes, generated artifacts)
Agent does NOT mention red-level instructions (warm color tones, rose)
Agent does NOT mention blue-level instructions (cool color tones, violet)
